promptDescription: - **nodetrix** — explore a network/graph with community structure: tightly-connected groups are drawn as adjacency-matrix blocks and the sparse links between groups as a node-link layout, avoiding the hairball a plain force diagram produces on dense data. Communities are detected automatically, so you only supply nodes + links. **Use lazy SQL** for non-trivial datasets — return one row per edge with `source`, `target`, `value` columns; the node list is derived automatically. Spec: (lazy SQL form) { "type":"nodetrix", "title":"...", "sql":"SELECT s.ip_address AS source, d.ip_address AS target, COUNT(*) AS value FROM net90.traffic_90min t JOIN net90.ip_map s ON s.ip_id=t.src_ip JOIN net90.ip_map d ON d.ip_id=t.dst_ip GROUP BY 1,2" }. Spec: (inline form, small examples only) { "type":"nodetrix", "title":"...", "nodes":[{"name":"A"},{"name":"B"},{"name":"C"}], "links":[{"source":0,"target":1,"value":1}], "clusters":[[0,1,2]] }. `clusters` is optional (auto-detected when omitted); link source/target may be node indices or node names. Optional `maxMatrices` (number) keeps only the N largest communities as matrix blocks and renders the rest as individual nodes; optional `dropOrphans` (boolean) hides individual nodes that have no edge to any kept matrix community — together they tame fragmented hub-and-spoke graphs. Render-side note: a right-hand control panel lets the user switch the grouping algorithm (Louvain, Label Prop, Components, or provided clusters) and tune visual settings (cell size, max matrix size, max matrices, link opacity, labels, drop-orphans); clicking any matrix cell, matrix block, or single node shows its details, and blocks are draggable with scroll-to-zoom.
